r2.data_catalog.create.title: Create Data Catalog
r2.data_catalog.create.configure.compact: Enable automatic file compaction to optimize table files for better query performance.
r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance_setup: Catalog created successfully, but maintenance (compaction/snapshot expiration) could not be configured automatically. You can configure maintenance in the Settings tab.
r2.data_catalog.create.bucket.title: Name your catalog
r2.data_catalog.create.bucket.descriptio: Enter a name for your R2 Data Catalog. If an R2 bucket doesn't already exist, we'll create one for you.

r2.data_catalog.create.bucket.hint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. 3-63 characters.

r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance.title: Configure table maintenance
r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance.descr: Automated maintenance keeps your Iceberg tables performant. You can change these settings later.
r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance.snaps: Automatically remove old snapshots to reclaim storage space.
r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance.targe: Small files are merged until they reach this size.
r2.data_catalog.create.maintenance.snaps: Snapshots older than this are removed.
Always keep at least this many snapshots.

r2.data_catalog.docs_sidebar.title: Documentation
Compaction improves query performance by combining many small files created during data ingestion into fewer, larger files. During open beta, tables are compacted up to 2 GB once per hour.
r2.data_catalog.docs_sidebar.snapshot_ex: Snapshot expiration automatically removes old table snapshots to reduce metadata bloat and storage costs. Configure minimum snapshots to keep and maximum snapshot age.
r2.data_catalog.docs_sidebar.query_engin: R2 Data Catalog exposes a standard Iceberg REST catalog interface. Connect engines like Spark, Snowflake, PyIceberg, DuckDB, and ClickHouse using your Catalog URI and API token.
r2.data_catalog.docs_sidebar.getting_sta: Create an R2 bucket, enable Data Catalog, create an API token, then connect your preferred query engine to start creating and querying Iceberg tables.
r2.data_catalog.docs_sidebar.authenticat: Iceberg clients must authenticate with an R2 API token that has both R2 Data Catalog and R2 storage permissions. Use Admin Read & Write for full access.
r2.data_catalog.studio.title: R2 SQL Studio
r2.data_catalog.studio.token_setup.title: Enable R2 SQL Studio access
r2.data_catalog.studio.token_setup.descr: R2 SQL Studio needs a token to query your Iceberg tables.
r2.data_catalog.studio.token_setup.read_: This creates a token scoped to your account with R2 Data Catalog and R2 storage permissions. Your token is stored securely by Cloudflare and used to run your queries.
r2.data_catalog.studio.token_setup.error: Failed to create token. Please try again.
r2.data_catalog.studio.token_setup.check: Couldn't check for an existing credential. Please try again.
r2.data_catalog.overview_tab.no_recent_a: No namespaces or tables created yet.
r2.data_catalog.side_panel.next_steps.de: Learn how to connect Apache Spark, Snowflake, DuckDB, and other Iceberg engines to your catalog.
r2.data_catalog.settings.credential.desc: A credential is required for automated table maintenance. This token has the minimal scope necessary to perform compaction and snapshot expiration.
r2.data_catalog.settings.credential.upda: Paste a new API token to replace the existing credential. The token must have R2 Data Catalog and R2 Storage permissions.
A credential lets R2 SQL Studio query your Iceberg tables from the dashboard. The token is stored securely by Cloudflare and used to run your queries.
Paste a new API token to replace the existing credential. The token must have R2 SQL Read, R2 Data Catalog Read and Write, and R2 Storage Read and Write permissions.
r2.data_catalog.create.review.auto_crede: A credential with the minimal scope necessary for table maintenance will be automatically generated.
r2.data_catalog.settings.maintenance.mis: A credential is required for table maintenance to function. Generate one below or configure one in the Credential section.
